[Dwain Lingenfelter](/wiki/Dwain_Lingenfelter "Dwain Lingenfelter") wins the [leadership election](/wiki/Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party/Co-operative_Commonwealth_Federation_leadership_elections#2009_New_Democratic_Party_leadership_convention "Saskatchewan New Democratic Party/Co-operative Commonwealth Federation leadership elections") to succeed [Lorne Calvert](/wiki/Lorne_Calvert "Lorne Calvert").

The [2011 general election](/wiki/2011_Saskatchewan_general_election "2011 Saskatchewan general election") is held, reducing the NDP to nine seats. Dwain Lingenfelter loses [his seat](/wiki/Regina_Douglas_Park "Regina Douglas Park"), and resigns as NDP leader.

The provincial council of the Saskatchewan NDP elects [Regina Lakeview](/wiki/Regina_Lakeview "Regina Lakeview") MLA [John Nilson](/wiki/John_Nilson "John Nilson") as interim leader.[[2]](#cite_note-Nilson-3)

The party announces the date and rules for the 2013 leadership race, scheduling it for either February or March 2013. The party announces that it will use a [one member, one vote](/wiki/One_member,_one_vote "One member, one vote") system using a [preferential ballot](/wiki/Ranked_voting_systems "Ranked voting systems"). The party also announces tighter rules about how memberships can be purchased.[[3]](#cite_note-March102012-4)
The party announces that the leadership convention will be held on March 9, 2013, with a general convention scheduled to be held on March 8 and 10.[[4]](#cite_note-May42012-5)
First all-candidates debate is held in [Regina](/wiki/Regina,_Saskatchewan "Regina, Saskatchewan")[[5]](#cite_note-FirstDebate-6)

Third all-candidates debate is held in [Saskatoon](/wiki/Saskatoon "Saskatoon")[[7]](#cite_note-ThirdDebate-8)
Fourth all-candidates debate is held in [Swift Current](/wiki/Swift_Current "Swift Current")[[6]](#cite_note-Debates-7)
Fifth all-candidates debate is held in [Melfort](/wiki/Melfort,_Saskatchewan "Melfort, Saskatchewan")[[6]](#cite_note-Debates-7)

An [election for the leadership](/wiki/Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party/Co-operative_Commonwealth_Federation_leadership_elections "Saskatchewan New Democratic Party/Co-operative Commonwealth Federation leadership elections") of the [Saskatchewan New Democratic Party](/wiki/Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party "Saskatchewan New Democratic Party") was triggered on November 7, 2011 following [Dwain Lingenfelter's](/wiki/Dwain_Lingenfelter "Dwain Lingenfelter") resignation after losing [his seat](/wiki/Regina_Douglas_Park "Regina Douglas Park") in the [2011 election](/wiki/2011_Saskatchewan_general_election "2011 Saskatchewan general election"). The party selected its new leader on March 9, 2013, by a one-member one vote system held during a convention at [TCU Place](/wiki/TCU_Place "TCU Place") in [Saskatoon](/wiki/Saskatoon "Saskatoon").[[1]](#cite_note-2)

## Timeline

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2013_Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party_leadership_election&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Timeline")]

* **June 7, 2009**: [Dwain Lingenfelter](/wiki/Dwain_Lingenfelter "Dwain Lingenfelter") wins the [leadership election](/wiki/Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party/Co-operative_Commonwealth_Federation_leadership_elections#2009_New_Democratic_Party_leadership_convention "Saskatchewan New Democratic Party/Co-operative Commonwealth Federation leadership elections") to succeed [Lorne Calvert](/wiki/Lorne_Calvert "Lorne Calvert").
* **November 7, 2011**: The [2011 general election](/wiki/2011_Saskatchewan_general_election "2011 Saskatchewan general election") is held, reducing the NDP to nine seats. Dwain Lingenfelter loses [his seat](/wiki/Regina_Douglas_Park "Regina Douglas Park"), and resigns as NDP leader.
* **November 19, 2011**: The provincial council of the Saskatchewan NDP elects [Regina Lakeview](/wiki/Regina_Lakeview "Regina Lakeview") MLA [John Nilson](/wiki/John_Nilson "John Nilson") as interim leader.[[2]](#cite_note-Nilson-3)
* **March 10, 2012**: The party announces the date and rules for the 2013 leadership race, scheduling it for either February or March 2013. The party announces that it will use a [one member, one vote](/wiki/One_member,_one_vote "One member, one vote") system using a [preferential ballot](/wiki/Ranked_voting_systems "Ranked voting systems"). The party also announces tighter rules about how memberships can be purchased.[[3]](#cite_note-March102012-4)
* **May 4, 2012**: The party announces that the leadership convention will be held on March 9, 2013, with a general convention scheduled to be held on March 8 and 10.[[4]](#cite_note-May42012-5)
* **November 18, 2012**: First all-candidates debate is held in [Regina](/wiki/Regina,_Saskatchewan "Regina, Saskatchewan")[[5]](#cite_note-FirstDebate-6)
* **November 22, 2012**: Second all-candidates debate is held in [Humboldt](/wiki/Humboldt,_Saskatchewan "Humboldt, Saskatchewan")[[6]](#cite_note-Debates-7)
* **November 24, 2012**: Third all-candidates debate is held in [Saskatoon](/wiki/Saskatoon "Saskatoon")[[7]](#cite_note-ThirdDebate-8)
* **November 29, 2012**: Fourth all-candidates debate is held in [Swift Current](/wiki/Swift_Current "Swift Current")[[6]](#cite_note-Debates-7)
* **December 1, 2012**: Fifth all-candidates debate is held in [Melfort](/wiki/Melfort,_Saskatchewan "Melfort, Saskatchewan")[[6]](#cite_note-Debates-7)
* **January 12, 2013**: Sixth all-candidates debate will be held in [Rosetown](/wiki/Rosetown,_Saskatchewan "Rosetown, Saskatchewan")[[8]](#cite_note-2013Debates-9)
* **January 19, 2013**: Seventh all-candidates debate will be held in [Weyburn](/wiki/Weyburn "Weyburn")[[8]](#cite_note-2013Debates-9)
* **January 24, 2013**: Eighth all-candidates debate will be held in [North Battleford](/wiki/North_Battleford "North Battleford")[[8]](#cite_note-2013Debates-9)
* **January 26, 2013**: Deadline for new members to sign-up and be eligible to vote at the leadership convention.[[9]](#cite_note-Deadline-10)
* **January 31, 2013**: Ninth all-candidates debate will be held in [Yorkton](/wiki/Yorkton "Yorkton")[[8]](#cite_note-2013Debates-9)
* **February 7, 2013**: Tenth all-candidates debate will be held in [Prince Albert](/wiki/Prince_Albert,_Saskatchewan "Prince Albert, Saskatchewan")[[8]](#cite_note-2013Debates-9)
* **February 9, 2013**: Eleventh all-candidates debate will be held in [Regina](/wiki/Regina,_Saskatchewan "Regina, Saskatchewan")[[8]](#cite_note-2013Debates-9)
* **February 13, 2013**: Twelfth all-candidates debate will be held in [Moose Jaw](/wiki/Moose_Jaw "Moose Jaw")[[8]](#cite_note-2013Debates-9)
* **February 16, 2013**: Final all-candidates debate will be held in [Saskatoon](/wiki/Saskatoon "Saskatoon")[[8]](#cite_note-2013Debates-9)
* **March 8–10, 2013**: Convention will be held at [TCU Place](/wiki/TCU_Place "TCU Place") in [Saskatoon](/wiki/Saskatoon "Saskatoon").
* **March 9, 2013**: Last day of voting. Results announced at leadership convention.
